architecture.summary: GDQA is a question-answering system that combines a geospatial data knowledge graph (GDKG) with large language models (LLMs) to enable natural language-based data discovery. The system first constructs GDKG by aggregating data from five major portals and using an information extraction model to automatically identify and structure knowledge from unstructured text. It then implements Spatio-Temporal Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs (STRKG), which enables LLMs to reason and explore over GDKG to answer complex geoscientific queries. The architecture bridges domain-specific knowledge from the structured knowledge graph with the flexibility and natural language understanding capabilities of LLMs.
trained_components: information extraction model for GDKG construction
Spatio-Temporal Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs (STRKG) method

biblio.title: A question-answering framework for geospatial data retrieval enhanced by a knowledge graph and large language models

claims.statement: GDKG is constructed by aggregating data from five major portals and employing an information extraction model to automatically identify and structure fine-grained knowledge from unstructured texts
Spatio-Temporal Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs (STRKG) enables LLMs to perform reasoning and exploration over GDKG, enhancing their ability to understand and respond to complex geoscientific queries
GDQA achieves superior performance in both accuracy and efficiency compared to baseline approaches, significantly reducing time and token costs
